title: Aglaia simplicifolia
intro: Aglaia simplicifolia, also known as the Philippine aglaia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce pain, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Aglaia simplicifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess significant pharmacological activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, as well as to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Aglaia simplicifolia is classified within the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family. This family is part of the order Sapindales, which includes other related plant families such as Rutaceae and Anacardiaceae. The genus Aglaia is a part of the family Meliaceae and is native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. Aglaia simplicifolia is one of the species within this genus and is found in various countries, including Indonesia and the Philippines. The classification of Aglaia simplicifolia is further detailed in botanical studies and taxonomic research.
uses: Aglaia simplicifolia is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands. Its extracts have been found to possess various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
